channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: US renews Russia GL authorising some transactions with the Russian Central Bank, National Wealth Funds & Finance Ministry until 9 October 2026
item.description: The US has issued General License 13R to continue to authorise transactions involving the Russian Central Bank, National Wealth Fund and Ministry of Finance which would otherwise be prohibited by Dir

item.title: Latvian Financial Intelligence Unit publishes guidance on assessing ‘control’ exercised by designated public officials
item.description: The Latvian Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) has published updated guidance on the meaning and application of the ‘control test’ under EU targeted financial sanctions, where control might be exercise

item.title: CJEU rejects Nathalie Yamb’s appeal against refusal of interim measures in Russia hybrid threats listing challenge
item.description: The European Court of Justice has dismissed an appeal by Nathalie Yamb against a General Court order refusing to suspend her EU sanctions listing pending final hearing – Yamb v Council, C-29/26 P(R).

item.title: National Crime Agency applies to confiscate funds of Russia sanctioned person
item.description: The UK's National Crime Agency (NCA) has brought a civil forfeiture application in the Westminster Magistrates’ Court to confiscate permanently funds worth $357,000 from UK-sanctioned Sergei Sergeevic

item.title: ECtHR finds no Article 8 violation over Ukrainian ex-MP’s inclusion on Swiss asset-freeze list
item.description: The European Court of Human Rights has said Switzerland did not violate a former Ukrainian MP’s right to privacy protected by the European Convention on Human rights by keeping his name on a public li

item.title: UK delists oil tanker ‘Zangazur’ from list of specified ships
item.description: The UK has delisted oil tanker ‘Zangazur’ (IMO 9420617) from its list of specified Russian ships. The tanker was specified in May 2025 under the UK’s Russia (Sanctions) (EU Exit) Regulations 2025 a

item.title: AG Biondi opinion on Reg 269 “leading businessman” criterion (g) in German Khan delisting appeal to ECJ
item.description: Advocate General Biondi (the Italian AG at the CJEU) has published his opinion in Case C-530/25 P, German Khan’s appeal to the CJEU against the General Court’s judgment rejecting his application to an

item.title: US revokes General Licence X which had authorised sale of Iranian oil
item.description: The US has revoked Iran-related General License X authorising the sale and delivery of Iranian crude oil (which was due to expire on 21 August 2026) and has replaced it with General License X1 in resp

item.title: Ongoing claims by VTB Capital in the English Commercial Court arising out of UK and EU sanctions
item.description: In 2025 VTB Capital plc (the UK subsidiary of Russian VTB Bank PJSC, which entered administration in 2022) issued claims against 3 investment institutions, for their actions following the freeze of VT

item.title: EU publishes compilation of national dual-use control lists
item.description: The European Commission has published a compilation of national control lists under Article 9(4) of Regulation (EU) 2021/821, the EU dual-use regulation. Member States notify these national lists to t
